_README: Allowlist for the frontend `npm audit` CI gate (scripts/audit-check.mjs).
An entry here SUPPRESSES a high/critical advisory that would otherwise fail CI.
1. Fixing beats suppressing. Only add an entry when no non-breaking upgrade clears it.
2. `reason` must be a concrete, checkable not-applicable argument -- which vulnerable
code path exists and why THIS app never reaches it. 'It is noisy' is not a reason.
3. `remove_when` states the condition that retires the entry.
4. A stale entry (matches no current advisory) prints a non-fatal WARNING -- delete it.

advisories.title: React Router: RSC Mode CSRF Bypass Allows Action Execution Before 400 Response
reason: The vulnerability is reachable ONLY in React Router's RSC (React Server Components) mode,
where a server action can execute before the framework returns its 400 CSRF response.
This app has no RSC mode and no server at all in front of the router. Verified:
- no RSC imports anywhere in frontend/src (`react-router/rsc`, `unstable_RSC`, `RSCErrorHandler`);
- the router is a plain client-side <BrowserRouter> in frontend/src/App.tsx;
- the build is a client-only `vite build` with no ssr configuration.
There is therefore no server action to execute and no CSRF boundary to bypass.
Also flagged transitively on `react-router-dom`, which has no advisory of its own.
advisories.remove_when: react-router is upgraded to >=8.3.0, which requires the v8 migration that drops react-router-dom (no v8 of react-router-dom exists; the package folds into react-router in v8, so clearing this means rewriting imports across ~59 pages).
advisories.do_not: Never run `npm audit fix --force` for this: it resolves react-router-dom DOWN to 7.11.0 and reintroduces four advisories that were patched in 7.18.0.
